sections.paragraph:
ἔα (Aeschyl. et al. in Attic poets; rare in prose: Pla., Prot. 314d; Epict. 2, 24, 22; 3, 20, 5; Job 15:16; 25:6) an exclamatory particle expressing surprise or displeasure, ah!, ha! Mk 1:24 v.l.; Lk 4:34. Some connection w. ἔα, impv. of ἐάω, let alone! seems likely in 1 Cl, and poss. in Mk and Lk (cp. Vulg. and NRSV mng., Lk 4:34). S. ἐάω 2.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

impv. impv. = imperative

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

poss. poss. = possible

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Vulg. Vulg. = Vulgate

NRSV NRSV = New Revised Standard Version of the NT—List 6

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 267
